notes: Springtime druid focused on the recovery of blighted and barren lands. Travels with investigative teams to document and reverse environmental damage left behind by Weirding events.
personality: I speak to plants before I touch them. Every barren patch of earth is simply waiting for the right conditions — you have to listen before you can help.
ideals: Renewal. Every scorched field and every dead tree is a promise waiting to be kept. Nothing is truly gone until the last seed is gone.
bonds: A blight carved a scar through the heart of the Hurstwold years ago. I will not stop until that wound has healed, no matter how long it takes.
flaws: I can become so absorbed in a dying plant or blighted soil that I lose track of everything happening around me — including things that actually need my attention.

time_to_shine.summary: Petal is irreplaceable anywhere the natural world needs to be read, healed, or reasoned with. WIS 17 and a background built around environmental investigation mean she notices what others walk past and understands what others can't name. She does not rush these moments. The land has been waiting longer than anyone in the party.

moments.context: The party enters a blighted or damaged area and needs to understand what happened
moments.text: Petal crouches, presses a hand to the soil, and begins. She speaks to what's left before she asks anyone else a question. WIS 17 Perception plus Hurstwold Warden training means she can date the damage, identify the cause, and estimate what it will take to reverse it — all before the party has finished looking around. She will not be rushed through this. They learn to wait.

moments.context: A party member is poisoned, diseased, or suffering a condition that combat can't fix
moments.text: Lesser Restoration. She identifies the condition, selects the right application, and clears it. She notes the symptoms in her mental catalogue of blight-adjacent effects. If she's seen this before, she says so. If she hasn't, she files it and says that too. Either way, the party member is back on their feet.

moments.context: The party needs to find a trail, a passage, or something hidden in natural terrain
moments.text: Druidcraft tells her which way the wind has been disturbed. WIS 17 Perception finds the bent stems, the disturbed soil, the absence of birds in a space that should have them. She does not track by following footprints. She tracks by understanding what the land remembers. She has, on more than one occasion, found something the party wasn't looking for.

moments.context: A blighted or dying plant, tree, or ecosystem could be saved with intervention
moments.text: This is the moment Petal exists for. She will spend as long as it takes. The party has learned not to set a time limit on this. Whatever she does here — tending, speaking, coaxing growth back from the edge — is not performed for an audience. She is simply doing the work. It is the most focused anyone will ever see her.

combat_tactics.role: Battlefield shaper with serious action-economy at level 5. Conjure Animals floods the field with creatures, Call Lightning fires every turn for free, and WIS 17 makes every save target DC 14 — hard to shake off.

tactics.text: 3rd level conjuration. Summon 8 wolves (or equivalent CR 1/4 beasts) or 4 CR 1/2 beasts or 2 CR 1 beasts. Each wolf: 11 HP, +4 to hit, 2d4+2 pierce + prone on hit (DC 11 STR save). Eight wolves attacking per round = massive action economy. Concentration for up to 1 hour. This alone can handle most encounters.

3rd level, concentration, 1 hour outdoors. Summon a storm: bonus action each turn to call a bolt (30-ft radius cylinder, 60-ft range) — 3d10 lightning, DC 14 DEX save for half. No action cost after casting — Petal can Shillelagh swing and still call lightning as a bonus action. Devastating sustained damage for outdoor encounters.

When Conjure Animals is overkill or indoors: Entangle (WIS save DC 14, restrained in 20-ft square), then Shillelagh bonus action (WIS for attack/damage, d8). Healing Word on the bonus action when animals handle the Entangle follow-up. WIS 17 makes the DC 14 save meaningful even for well-statted enemies.

Circle of the Land: recover spell slots totaling up to 2 levels on a short rest. Use after big fights to restore one 2nd-level slot (Barkskin, Entangle upcast) or two 1st-level slots (Cure Wounds, Healing Word). The 3rd-level slots — Conjure Animals, Call Lightning — are long-rest resources. Plan accordingly.
